schemaVersion.description: 0.1 and 0.2 coexist during the copy-verify-delete migration window (Decision #13). Current plan/initiative writers prefer 0.1; 0.2 is accepted for explicitly upgraded files and is additive-optional over 0.1 (mutation/manual/task.evidence fields). A bare const would instantly invalidate every live 0.1 file (gitignored, not git-restorable), so the enum is required for coexistence.

isoTimestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp with explicit Z or ±HH:MM offset.

slug.description: kebab-case identifier. Lowercase, starts with letter, 2-64 chars.

provenance.description: Records when and how an item (task or phase) was added AFTER the initial materialization of its container. Absent on items that shipped in the original plan/initiative; present on items added mid-execution via the agent-proposes / user-invokes flow described in skills/core/project-status.md. When present on a task/phase, `context` becomes mandatory.

surfacedDuring.description: Either `<initiative-slug>/<task-id>` (most common: emerged while working on a specific task) or `<initiative-slug>` (emerged during initiative-level work) or a short free-form note when no anchor is appropriate.

surfacedBy.description: Who surfaced the item — human in conversation, or AI that noticed it while working.

originalPhaseId.description: For tasks moved cross-phase via reanchor: the phase the task originally belonged to.

context.description: Captures the WHY of an item that wasn't part of the original materialization. `solves` and `trigger` answer 'what problem does this address?' and 'what made it surface?'. `ratifiedAt` records that the human explicitly approved this articulation (vs. an agent-only draft). Mandatory on every `parked` and `emerged` entry, and on any task/phase that also carries `provenance`. The mandatory-ratify flow lives in skills/core/project-status.md under 'Emergent work — proposal / ratify / commit pattern'.

solves.description: One-sentence statement of the problem this item addresses. Read first when re-evaluating the backlog — if `solves` no longer applies, the item is stale.

trigger.description: What caused the item to surface — an incident, a code-review finding, a test failure, an observation while implementing another task, a user request. Concrete, not generic.

assumesStillValid.description: Premises that, if invalidated, render the item moot. Used by re-evaluation prompts and `lastReviewedAt`-aging banners — when any item in this list is no longer true, the item should be re-ratified or archived.

ratifiedAt.description: Timestamp of the explicit user confirmation (`ratify` command or equivalent paste-back). Distinct from `provenance.surfacedAt` — a thing can be surfaced by the agent at T1 and only ratified by the human at T2.

ratifiedBy.description: Almost always `human`. `ai-with-explicit-user-confirm` covers the narrow case where the user authorized the agent (mid-conversation) to ratify on their behalf — recorded so audits can distinguish.

lastReviewedAt.description: Last time the item's `solves` and `assumesStillValid` were re-checked against current reality. Initialized to `ratifiedAt` on creation; updated by future `re-ratify` runs. Aging thresholds in `.atomic-skills/status/config.json`.

demoCommand.description: 0.2 (manual gate): command that brings the change to a demoable state (run before generating the acceptance script). If absent, the criterion is only manually-checkable as-is.

fallbackKind.description: 0.2 (manual gate): the user-visible surface the manual check exercises, used to pick the acceptance-script template when no demoCommand applies.

steps.description: 0.2 (manual gate): generated imperative Given/When/Then acceptance steps (<10), concrete data.

expected.description: 0.2 (manual gate): the observable result(s) the user must confirm for each EXPECT step.

data.description: 0.2 (manual gate): concrete input data the acceptance script uses (so the check is reproducible, not abstract).

exitCriterion.description: Strict gate shape. It deliberately has no lastUpdated field; reconcile acknowledges a still-open criterion by advancing the containing initiative's lastUpdated detection anchor.

verifierLabel.description: Derived (scripts/compute-rollups.js): one-line human label of `verifier` (kind + key arg, truncated) for the dashboard. Never hand-authored; recomputed alongside rollups.

evidenceSummary.description: Derived (scripts/compute-rollups.js): one-line summary of `evidence`/`deferredReason` for the dashboard; '' (omitted) while pending. Never hand-authored; recomputed alongside rollups.

evidence.description: Captured result of running the criterion's verifier. Written by the project-status skill's phase-done workflow. Read by aiDeck (when present) to render gate evidence.

verifiedCommit.description: Full git object id of the exact HEAD against which this evidence was produced. Optional for legacy records; required at hardened phase-close boundaries.

passed.description: True iff the verifier produced the expected result. Stored even when status='met' to disambiguate user-overrides from verified passes.

exitCode.description: Shell verifier: actual exit code observed.

rowCount.description: Query verifier: row count observed (kind:query is DEFERRED-BY-DESIGN in this line; never reaches met without a real rowCount).

testsCollected.description: 0.2 (test verifier): number of tests the runner actually collected/ran, parsed from output. A pattern that matched 0 tests must NOT reach status:met (R-XAGENT-07 false-green guard).

outputSummary.description: Brief excerpt of verifier output (truncated to ~500 chars). For manual verifier, the user's confirmation note.

mutation.description: 0.2 (test verifier, G9 mutation-kill): records a behavioral mutation injected at a recorded file:line that a test must catch. A surviving behavioral mutant = tautological/mock-only test = HARD FAIL. Maps to a NAMED acceptance criterion + adversarial pick (fork-3).

target.description: file:line where the behavioral mutation was injected.

change.description: the behavioral mutation applied (e.g. flipped a comparison, off-by-one).

killedBy.description: the test(s) that went RED on the mutation (empty = surviving mutant = fail).

archived.description: `archived` prerequisites block by default; `resolved` records an explicit decision that archiving satisfied this edge.

summary.description: Concise one-line human summary of what this phase does — distinct from the longer, technical `goal`. Authored + user-validated when phases are generated (post-decompose annotation; decompose.js format stays frozen per R-ORCH-10). Shown on the dashboard Home timeline. Helps a dev recall each phase + validates the AI's interpretation.

businessIntent.description: F0/T-001 (phase-materialization): the load-bearing business spine — value/workflow/rules/outOfScope/doneWhen — authored + user-validated when the phase is materialized (D1 strong-lazy), persisted on both the plan phase descriptor and the initiative file (F-001), and surfaced for the materialize gate (D4). Optional + additive (mirrors `summary`): an absent block means a not-yet-activated/legacy surface. `derived[]` captures open questions + tentative answers (never gated). scripts/find-missing-business-intent.js (T-003) hard-blocks a materialized phase missing any spine field.

spawnedPlans.description: Fork parent→child origin edge (rung 7.5): child plan slugs forked out of THIS phase. Bidirectional with each child's top-level `spawnedFrom`. Additive — phases without forks omit it. Mirrors aideck phaseDescriptorSchema.spawnedPlans and the inline field migrated from legacy links.json. Durable edge fields are child slugs only; fork rationale `context`/`provenance` is not persisted on this edge.

reviewGate.description: 0.2 (G2 phase-review gate): the outcome of phase-done's review-code gate for THIS phase, written before the phase is set 'done' (project-transitions.md phase-done step 6/8). Shape only here; the cross-field honesty conditional (done + status:passed ⟹ `at` sha present; done + status:skipped ⟹ `reason` present) is enforced by scripts/validate-state.js GATE-R3, NOT the schema — same reason GATE-R2 lives in JS (keeps the aiDeck TS mirror additive-optional). An absent block on a legacy 'done' phase is tolerated (GATE-R2-consistent); `verify` WARNs on it.

status.description: passed = review-code ran and blocker/critical findings were resolved; skipped = --skip-review with a recorded reason.

mode.description: which review mode ran (a destructive phase runs 'both' per G5).

at.description: the commit sha the review concluded against (post-fix HEAD). Required-when-passed by GATE-R3.

reviewFile.description: path to the persisted review file, when one was written (e.g. .atomic-skills/reviews/<…>.md).

reason.description: why review was skipped. Required-when-skipped by GATE-R3.

allOf.description: Phases inserted mid-plan (with provenance) must articulate WHY via context. Original-materialization phases live without context — their narrative is the plan body.

summary.description: Concise one-line human summary of what this task does — distinct from the label `title` and the longer `description`. Authored + user-validated at decompose (and whenever a task is created mid-execution); shown on the dashboard Home (Agora task table) and the Initiative-detail tasks table. Generated in the install-configured language; backfill detector = scripts/find-missing-task-summaries.js. Never hand-authored as a one-off outside the skill loop (skills/core/project.md → Task summaries).

weight.description: Complexity proxy for the earned-value burn-up (F2). Optional; absent ⇒ treated as 1 (count-burnup degradation). AI-authored at decompose (Stage 6 of project-create-plan.md) from structural signals (# of acceptance, Files, scopeBoundary, verifier kind); decompose.js stays frozen (R-ORCH-10). Backfill detector = scripts/find-unweighted-tasks.js. Summed into the weightDone/weightTotal rollups.

scopeBoundary.description: Explicit exclusions — what this task must NOT do. Prevents scope creep at the task level.

acceptance.description: Executable acceptance criteria in it()-style assertions. Max 5 items.

evidence.description: 0.2 (F-B4): captured result of running this task's `verifier` on `done <task-id>`, reusing the exact exitCriterion.evidence shape. Replaces the v0.1 description-note string-laundering workaround so per-task verifier results are machine-enforceable (GATE-R2).

completionProvenance.description: Immutable task-close inputs persisted by the recoverable done transaction. Optional for backward readability; markerless repair fails closed when a historical task lacks this authority.

allOf.description: Tasks added mid-execution (with provenance) must articulate WHY via context. Original-materialization tasks live without context — their narrative is the plan/initiative body.

plan.title: atomic-skills Plan frontmatter (schemaVersion 0.1/0.2)
plan.description: Validates the YAML frontmatter of nested `.atomic-skills/projects/<project-id>/<plan-slug>/plan.md` files, with legacy flat `.atomic-skills/plans/<slug>.md` accepted during migration. Current plan writers prefer schemaVersion 0.1; 0.2 remains accepted for explicitly upgraded files. Mirrors aideck/src/schemas/project-status.ts:Plan. The `narrative` field of the TypeScript Plan interface lives in the markdown body, NOT frontmatter — so it is not declared here.

stateIntegrityHardening.description: Forward-only opt-in for strict project/plan/phase identity, terminality and uniqueness cross-validation. Legacy plans remain readable until migrated and explicitly hardened.

successorBarriers.description: Descriptor-only activations that must revalidate a historical receipt and a prerequisite phase close commit through scripts/materialize-state.js.

closedAtHardening.description: F4/T-003: opts this plan into the closedAt forward-only hard-gate. Written ONCE by scripts/harden-closedat.js. grandfatheredTaskIds = the done-without-closedAt tasks alive at the flip instant (exempt); every later done task must record closedAt. Never implies a retroactive closedAt (P3).

planActive.description: Derived focus marker (scripts/reconcile-focus.js): true iff status:'active'. Carried by the `phases` dataSource onto each phase row so the Home timeline scopes to active plans (the dashboard can't join). Never hand-authored.

planTitle.description: Derived denormalization (scripts/reconcile-focus.js): a copy of `title`, carried by the `phases` dataSource onto each phase row so a repeat-grouped widget can label the group with the human plan title instead of the slug (the dashboard can't join). Never hand-authored.

spawnedFrom.description: Fork child→parent origin edge (rung 7.5): points at the parent plan + the anchor phase it was extracted from. Bidirectional with the parent phase's `spawnedPlans`. Additive (distinct from `supersedes`, which is replacement): the parent consumes the child's result. Mirrors aideck spawnedFromSchema and the inline field migrated from legacy links.json. Durable edge fields are identity + mode only; fork rationale `context`/`provenance` is not persisted on this edge. `mode`: pause = parent waits for the child; parallel = both active, each in its own worktree.

dependsOnPlans.description: Operational plan dependency edges persisted on the blocked plan. Each entry points at a prerequisite plan in the same project. This is separate from `spawnedFrom` / `phases[].spawnedPlans`, which describe origin only.

initiative.title: atomic-skills Initiative frontmatter (schemaVersion 0.1/0.2)
initiative.description: Validates the YAML frontmatter of nested phase files at `.atomic-skills/projects/<project-id>/<plan-slug>/phases/f<N>-*.md`, with legacy flat `.atomic-skills/initiatives/<slug>.md` accepted during migration. Current initiative writers prefer schemaVersion 0.1; 0.2 remains accepted for explicitly upgraded files. Mirrors aideck/src/schemas/project-status.ts:Initiative + Task. The `body` field of the TypeScript Initiative interface lives in the markdown body, NOT frontmatter — so it is not declared here. Standalone initiatives are represented as a degenerate one-phase plan in the nested layout; legacy flat standalone files omit `parentPlan` and `phaseId`.

summary.description: Concise one-line human summary of what this phase does (mirrors the plan phase descriptor's `summary`). Authored + user-validated at phase generation; shown on the dashboard Home (Agora callout).

businessIntent.description: F0/T-001 (phase-materialization): the load-bearing business spine — value/workflow/rules/outOfScope/doneWhen — authored + user-validated when the phase is materialized (D1 strong-lazy), persisted on both the plan phase descriptor and the initiative file (F-001), and surfaced for the materialize gate (D4). Optional + additive (mirrors `summary`): an absent block means a not-yet-activated/legacy surface. `derived[]` captures open questions + tentative answers (never gated). scripts/find-missing-business-intent.js (T-003) hard-blocks a materialized phase missing any spine field.

startedCommit.description: C-3 (earned-value integrity): the immutable git HEAD SHA at phase activation, recorded so phase-done actuals compute from a rebase/squash/amend-proof commit anchor instead of the fragile `started` committer-date heuristic. Optional + additive: absent on legacy phases, which degrade to the date heuristic. Consumed by scripts/append-completion.js computePhaseActuals via `--actuals-since-commit`.

tasksDone.description: Rollup precomputed by the project-status skill: count of tasks with status 'done'. Powers dashboard progress meters (aiDeck stays read-in-place / no compute engine). Recompute on every task mutation; see scripts/compute-rollups.js.

weightDone.description: Rollup precomputed by scripts/compute-rollups.js: sum of weight (default 1 per weightless task) over tasks with status 'done'. Weighted mirror of tasksDone for the earned-value burn-up (F2).

weightTotal.description: Rollup: sum of weight (default 1 per weightless task) over all tasks. Weighted mirror of tasksTotal.

gatesMet.description: Rollup: count of exitGates with status 'met'.

planActive.description: Derived focus marker (scripts/reconcile-focus.js): true iff this initiative's parent plan has status 'active'. Scopes the Home timeline to active plans (the dashboard can't join plan→initiative). Never hand-authored.

current.description: Derived focus marker (scripts/reconcile-focus.js): true iff planActive AND this is the plan's currentPhase — i.e. the single 'AGORA' phase. Powers the Home focus callout. Never hand-authored.

planTitle.description: Derived denormalization (scripts/reconcile-focus.js): the parent plan's `title`. Lets a repeat-grouped widget (grouped by the parentPlan slug) label the group with the human plan title instead of the slug (the dashboard can't join plan→initiative). Never hand-authored.
